tracker_summary.invention: The Transformer architecture — a neural network design relying entirely on self-attention mechanisms, eliminating recurrence and convolution
tracker_summary.inventor: Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, Jakob Uszkoreit, Llion Jones, Aidan N. Gomez, Lukasz Kaiser, Illia Polosukhin (Google Brain / Google Research)
tracker_summary.current_use: Foundation of virtually every large language model: GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, Mistral, and thousands of derivative systems
tracker_summary.initial_funding_source: Google LLC (internal research budget, Google Brain team)
tracker_summary.initial_funding_reason: Improve Google Translate's neural machine translation quality and computational efficiency

the_x: The need to improve Google Translate — a commercial product serving hundreds of millions of users daily, funded by Google's advertising revenue
the_y: The Transformer architecture: the universal substrate of the modern AI revolution, enabling GPT, BERT, Claude, Gemini, and the entire large language model ecosystem
summary: In 2017, a team of eight researchers at Google published a 15-page paper titled 'Attention Is All You Need' with the modest goal of making machine translation faster. They had no idea they were writing the foundational document of a technological revolution. But the lineage runs much deeper — through two devastating AI winters, multiple funding collapses, and decades of academic unfashionability. The chain runs from McCulloch and Pitts in 1943, through the brutal Minsky-Papert critique that nearly killed the field, through backpropagation's revival, through LSTMs, through attention mechanisms — and finally to eight engineers who were just trying to make a translation product work better.

chain.who: Warren McCulloch, Walter Pitts; Frank Rosenblatt
chain.where: University of Chicago; Cornell Aeronautical Laboratory
chain.what_they_were_doing: McCulloch and Pitts modeled the neuron mathematically; Rosenblatt built the first trainable network for the U.S. Navy.
chain.the_jump: Rosenblatt added a learning rule — the first proto-gradient-descent, the seed of everything that followed.

chain.what_they_were_doing: Published 'Perceptrons,' proving single-layer nets couldn't solve XOR — neural network funding collapsed for over a decade.
chain.the_jump: Neural network research became professionally toxic. Graduate students were advised against the field.
chain.funding: N/A — DARPA dramatically reduced neural network grants

chain.who: Paul Werbos; David Rumelhart, Geoffrey Hinton, Ronald Williams
chain.where: Harvard; UC San Diego; Carnegie Mellon
chain.what_they_were_doing: Rumelhart, Hinton, and Williams published backpropagation in Nature, making multi-layer networks trainable for the first time.
chain.the_jump: Backpropagation provided the missing ingredient for deep networks and triggered an explosion of renewed interest.

chain.what_they_were_doing: Published Long Short-Term Memory, solving the vanishing gradient problem with gating mechanisms.
chain.the_jump: LSTMs kept neural networks alive through the second AI winter — without them, no trained researchers exist to build what came next.

chain.who: Geoffrey Hinton, Yann LeCun, Yoshua Bengio; Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton
chain.where: University of Toronto; NYU; Université de Montréal
chain.what_they_were_doing: CIFAR united the three researchers during the AI winter; AlexNet won ImageNet 2012 by a margin that ended all debate.
chain.the_jump: AlexNet trained on two $500 GPUs crushed every competitor. Every major tech company immediately began aggressively hiring deep learning researchers.
chain.funding: CIFAR; NSERC; Canadian Foundation for Innovation

chain.who: Sutskever, Vinyals, Le; Bahdanau, Cho, Bengio
chain.what_they_were_doing: Seq2seq compressed sentences into fixed vectors; Bahdanau attention let the decoder dynamically weight each input word instead.
chain.the_jump: Bahdanau attention is the direct ancestor of self-attention in the Transformer. The core idea survived essentially unchanged into 2017.

chain.who: Google NMT team; Ashish Vaswani et al.
chain.where: Google Brain, Mountain View and New York
chain.what_they_were_doing: GNMT deployed to 500M daily users but was too slow to parallelize; Vaswani's team eliminated recurrence entirely with multi-head self-attention.
chain.the_jump: The question 'how do we make Google Translate faster?' accidentally produced the substrate of the entire modern AI revolution.

chain.who: OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Anthropic, Meta AI, hundreds more
chain.where: San Francisco; London; Mountain View; globally
chain.what_they_were_doing: BERT, GPT-1 through GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA — every major AI system built on the Transformer.
chain.the_jump: A 1943 theoretical neuron paper → two near-extinction AI winters → eight engineers fixing a translation product → the most consequential architecture of the 21st century.
chain.funding: Microsoft invested $13B in OpenAI; aggregate AI investment exceeded $500B by 2024

sources: McCulloch & Pitts (1943). Bulletin of Mathematical Biophysics, 5, 115–133. https://doi.org/10.1007/BF02478259
Hochreiter, S. (1991). Diploma thesis, TU Munich. http://people.idsia.ch/~juergen/SeppHochreiter1991ThesisAdvisorSchmidhuber.pdf
Wu et al. (2016). Google NMT. https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.08144
Vaswani et al. (2017). 'Attention Is All You Need.' NeurIPS 2017. https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762
